text: get started, key steps : Build required netCDF grids weather data. Extract required weather data user defined locations, duration time-step. Update data grids new data becomes available. steps outlined .

title: Gettings started with AWAPer
text: netCDF data grids created using function makeNetCDF_file. requires user input following: ncdfFilename define netCDF file created. Note, using default compression settings, meteorological variable requires ~5GB hard-drive storage full record (1900 now). Additionally, netCDF files stored locally, network, minimise time data extraction. updateFrom : start date data. Users often set default date .Date('1900-01-01') extract available data. updateTo : end date data. Users often set default date two days ago extract available data. urlPrecip etc define meteorological variables input netCDF data grids. Setting NA causes variable included netCDF data grids. See example build first netCDF data file.

title: Gettings started with AWAPer
text: netCDF data grids created, extract data using extractCatchmentData function. essential user inputs : ncdfFilename  file name pointing netCDF file. locations defining locations data xtracted. can full file name ESRI shape file points polygons shape file already imported using readShapeSpatial(). Either way shape file must long/lat (.e. projected), use ellipsoid GRS 80, first column must unique ID. Many user controls available control duration, time-step, spatial temporal aggregation (e.g. mean, sum, min max), meteorological variables required measure evapotranspiration. examples time-series extractions see . examples spatial data extractions see ).

what: Extract daily precipitation and PET data
title: Extract daily area weighted potential PET and precipitation and map
text: daily area weighted average precipitation PET extracted across two catchments. data extracted netCDF files ncdfFilename ncdfSolarFilename, dates extract.extract.. AWAPer variables extracted need calculation Morton’s PET. Note netCDF files must working directory full file path must given. Morton’s wet-environment areal evapotranspiration estimated (options available). use Morton’s CREA formulation defined setting ET.function variable ‘ET.MortonCRAE’. calculation areal wet environment PET defined setting Morton’s specific variable ET.Mortons.est ‘wet areal ET’ (note, options exist within AWAPer variables). Lastly, following McMahon et al, (2013), Morton’s PET calculated monthly time step (daily) improve reliability. monthly estimate interpolated daily. estimation ET uses evapotranspiration package. requires set constants, loaded follows. area weighted daily meteorological variables PET can calculated follows. Note, two months extracted , default method handle missing data anomalies (e.g. Tmin > Tmax) ET calculations automatically changed default . Next time series extracted daily precipitation PET catchment plotted. precipitation plots show area weighted estimate spatial variability day (+/- one standard deviation).
code: data(constants,package='Evapotranspiration') climateData.daily = extractCatchmentData(ncdfFilename=ncdfFilename,                       ncdfSolarFilename=ncdfSolarFilename,                       extractFrom=date.from, extractTo=date.to,                       locations=catchments, temporal.timestep = 'daily',                       temporal.function.name='sum',spatial.function.name='var',                       getTmin=T, getTmax=T, getVprp=T, getSolarrad=T, getET=T,                       ET.function='ET.MortonCRAE', ET.timestep = 'monthly',                       ET.Mortons.est='wet areal ET', ET.constants= constants) #> Extraction data summary: #>     NetCDF non-solar radiation climate data exists from 2010-07-01 to 2010-10-31 #>     NetCDF solar radiation data exists from 2010-07-01 to 2010-10-31 #>     Data will be extracted from  2010-07-01  to  2010-10-31  at  2  locations #>     WARNING: The extraction duration is < 2 years and getET = TRUE. #>              Hence, ET.missing_method and ET.abnormal_method is changed to "neighbouring average". #> Starting data extraction: #> ... Building catchment weights #> ... Extracted DEM elevations from AWS. #> Mosaicing & Projecting #> Note: Elevation units are in meters #> ... Starting to extract data across all locations: #> ... Calculating mean daily solar radiation <1990-1-1 #> ... Linearly interpolating gaps in daily solar. #> ... Calculating area weighted daily data. #>     Working on ET for location 1 of 2 #>     Working on ET for location 2 of 2 #> Data extraction FINISHED. #> Total run time (DD:HH:MM:SS): 00:00:00:27 par(mfrow=c(2,1), mar =  c(5, 7.5, 4, 2.7) + 0.1)  # Loop through each catchment and plot the daily precipitation and PET. for (i in 1:length(catchments$CatchID)) {    filt = climateData.daily$catchmentTemporal.sum$CatchID == catchments$CatchID[i]    # Convert year, month and day columns from extractions to a date.   climateData.daily.date = as.Date(paste0(climateData.daily$catchmentTemporal.sum$year[filt], "-",                            climateData.daily$catchmentTemporal.sum$month[filt], "-",                            climateData.daily$catchmentTemporal.sum$day[filt]))     # Plot precipitation and standard deviation against observations   # ---------------------------------------------------------   max.y = max(climateData.daily$catchmentTemporal.sum$precip_mm[filt] +         sqrt(climateData.daily$catchmentSpatial.var$precip_mm[filt]))    # Precipitation   plot(climateData.daily.date,         climateData.daily$catchmentTemporal.sum$precip_mm[filt],         type = "h", col = "#e31a1c", lwd = 3, mgp = c(2, 0.5, 0),         main=paste('Catchment ID',catchments$CatchID[i]),         ylim = c(0, 80), ylab = "", xlab = "2010", xaxs = "i", yaxt = "n", bty = "l", yaxs = "i")    axis(side = 2, mgp = c(2, 0.5, 0), line = 0.5, at = seq(from = 0, to = 80, by = 20),         labels = c("0", "20", "40", "60", "80mm"), col = "#e31a1c", col.axis = "#e31a1c")    # Standard deviation   for (j in 1:length(climateData.daily.date)) {     x.plot = rep(climateData.daily.date[j], 2)     y.plot = c(climateData.daily$catchmentTemporal.sum$precip_mm[filt][j] +         sqrt(climateData.daily$catchmentSpatial.var$precip_mm[filt][j]),         climateData.daily$catchmentTemporal.sum$precip_mm[filt][j] -         sqrt(climateData.daily$catchmentSpatial.var$precip_mm[filt][j]))     lines(x.plot, y.plot, col = "black", lwd = 1.2)   }    # Plot evap data.   par(new = TRUE)   plot(climateData.daily.date, climateData.daily$catchmentTemporal.sum$ET_mm[filt],       col = "#bc80bd", lwd = 2, ylab = "", ylim = c(0, 4), lty = 1,       xlab = "", xaxs = "i", yaxt = "n", xaxt = "n", type = "l", bty = "n", yaxs = "i")    axis(side = 2, line = 2.3, mgp = c(2, 0.5, 0), labels = c("0", "1", "2", "3", "4mm"),       at = seq(from = 0, to = 4, by = 1), col = "#bc80bd", col.axis = "#bc80bd")    legend("topleft", cex = 0.8, lwd = 2, bty = "n", inset = c(0.01, -0.01),       lty = c(1, 1), pch = c(NA, NA),       col = c("#e31a1c",  "#bc80bd"),       legend = c("Precipitation (bars +/- one standard dev.)", "Morton CRAE PET"), xpd = NA) }

[mm/month]",        xlab = "2010", xaxs = "i",        bty = "l", yaxs = "i")    lines(metData.date, metData.monthly.centroid$precip_mm[filt],       col = "#bc80bd", lwd = 1.2)    legend("bottomleft", cex = 0.8, lwd = 2, bty = "n", inset = c(0.01, -0.01),        lty = c(1, 1), pch = c(NA, NA),        col = c("#e31a1c",  "#bc80bd"),        legend = c("Areal weighted", "Centroid"), xpd = NA)    # PET   plot(metData.date,        metData.monthly.weighted$catchmentTemporal.sum$ET_mm[filt],        type = "l", col = "#e31a1c", lwd = 1.2, mgp = c(2, 0.5, 0),        main=paste('Catchment ID',catchments$CatchID[i]),        ylim = c(0, 120), ylab = "PET [mm/month]",        xlab = "2010", xaxs = "i",        bty = "l", yaxs = "i")    lines(metData.date, metData.monthly.centroid$ET_mm[filt],         col = "#bc80bd", lwd = 1.2)    # Deficit   precip.deficit.weighted =  metData.monthly.weighted$catchmentTemporal.sum$precip_mm[filt] -                     metData.monthly.weighted$catchmentTemporal.sum$ET_mm[filt]    plot(metData.date, precip.deficit.weighted,        type = "l", col = "#e31a1c", lwd = 1.2, mgp = c(2, 0.5, 0),        ylim = c(-25, 125), ylab = "P - PET [mm/month]",        xlab = "2010", xaxs = "i",        bty = "l", yaxs = "i")    precip.deficit.centroid =  metData.monthly.centroid$precip_mm[filt] -                     metData.monthly.centroid$ET_mm[filt]    lines(metData.date, precip.deficit.centroid, col = "#bc80bd", lwd = 1.2)    text(x = par("usr")[1]+5, y = par("usr")[3]+10,      labels = paste('Mean diff. =',round(mean(precip.deficit.weighted -               precip.deficit.centroid),1),'mm/month'),      adj = c(0, 0), cex=0.75) }

what: Catchment Area Weighted Climate Data Anywhere in Australia
title: Catchment Area Weighted Climate Data Anywhere in Australia
text: Getting rainfall evaporation time-series data essential first step many hydrological studies. often requires tedious task finding weather station sufficiently close study catchment. Rarely weather station inside catchment, even spatial variability rainfall evaporation throughout catchment must ignores - errors introduced (see example ). AWAPer eliminates challenges allowing efficient estimate area weighted (.e. catchment weighted) weather time-series data anywhere Australia time step. features include: Area weighted point precipitation, minimum maximum temperature, vapour pressure solar radiation anywhere Australia time-step. See examples . Maps catchment weather variables time showing spatial variability precipitation potential evapotranspiration (see examples ) Ten measures area weighted point evapotranspiration (see examples ) using Evaporation package, implementing McMahon et al, (2013), meteorological data sourced Australian Bureau Meteorology (BOM) ~5x5km gridded products (see ). package functions building compressed netCDF grids BOM data. Users generally build netCDF grids using historic data update required (see example ). package development funded Victorian Government Department Environment, Land, Water Planning Climate Water Initiate. details approach see function references journal paper Peterson et al. (2020).

title: extractCatchmentData extracts catchment average climate data from netCDF files containing Australian climate data. — extractCatchmentData
text: ncdfFilename full file name (string) netCDF file. ncdfSolarFilename full file name (string) netCDF file. extractFrom date string specifying start date data extraction. default "1900-1-1". extractTo date string specifying end date data extraction. default today's date YYYY-MM-DD. getPrecip logical variable extracting precipitation. Default TRUE. getTmin logical variable extracting Tmin. Default TRUE. getTmax logical variable extracting Tmax. Default TRUE. getVprp logical variable extracting vapour pressure. Default TRUE. getSolarrad logical variable extracting solar radiation. Default TRUE. getET logical variable calculating Morton's potential ET. Note, calculate set getTmin=T, getTmax=T, getVprp=T getSolarrad=T. Default TRUE. DEM.res zoom resolution land surface elevation. elevatr package used extract elevation (metres) AWS Open Data Terrain Tiles. input controls zoom resolution. Higher values increase accuracy, significantly slower. See details. Default 10. locations either full file name ESRI shape file points polygons (latter assumed catchment boundaries) shape file already imported using readShapeSpatial(). Either way shape file must long/lat (.e. projected), use ellipsoid GRS 80, first column must unique ID. temporal.timestep character string time step output data. options daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annual  user-defined index , say, water-years (see xts::period.apply). default daily. temporal.function.name character string function name applied aggregate daily data temporal.timestep. Note, NA values removed aggregation calculation. required consider writing function. default mean. spatial.function.name character string function name applied estimate daily spatial spread variable. NA "" locations polygon, spatial data returned. default var. interpMethod character string defining method interpolating gridded data (see raster::extract). options : 'simple', 'bilinear' ''. default ''. set interpolation 'simple' locations polygon(s) 'bilinear' locations points. ET.function character string evapotranspiration function used. methods can derived AWAP data ET.Abtew, ET.HargreavesSamani, ET.JensenHaise, ET.Makkink, ET.McGuinnessBordne, ET.MortonCRAE , ET.MortonCRWE, ET.Turc. Default ET.MortonCRAE. ET.Mortons.est character string type Morton's ET estimate. ET.MortonCRAE, options potential ET,wet areal ET actual areal ET. ET.MortonCRWE, options potential ET shallow lake ET. default potential ET. ET.Turc.humid logical variable Turc function using humid adjustment.See ET.Turc. now fixed F. ET.timestep character string evapotranpiration time step. Options daily,  monthly, annual options dependent upon chosen ET.function. default 'monthly'. ET.missing_method character string interpolation method missing variables required ET calculation. options 'monthly average', 'seasonal average', 'DoY average' 'neighbouring average'. Default 'DoY average' extraction duration less two years, default 'neighbouring average'. See ReadInputs ET.abnormal_method character string interpolation method abnormal variables required ET calculation (e.g. Tmin > Tmax). Options defaults ET.missing_method. See ReadInputs ET.constants list constants Evapotranspiration package required ET calculations. get data use command data(constants). Default list().

title: extractCatchmentData extracts catchment average climate data from netCDF files containing Australian climate data. — extractCatchmentData
code: # The example shows how to extract and save data. # For an additional example see \url{https://github.com/peterson-tim-j/AWAPer/blob/master/README.md} #--------------------------------------- library(sp)  # Set dates for building netCDFs and extracting data. # Note, to reduce runtime this is done only a fortnight (14 days). startDate = as.Date("2000-01-01","%Y-%m-%d") endDate = as.Date("2000-01-14","%Y-%m-%d")  # Set names for netCDF files. ncdfFilename = tempfile(fileext='.nc') ncdfSolarFilename = tempfile(fileext='.nc')  # Build netCDF grids and over a defined time period. # Only precip data is to be added to the netCDF files. # This is because the URLs for the other variables are set to zero. # \donttest{ file.names = makeNetCDF_file(ncdfFilename=ncdfFilename,              ncdfSolarFilename=ncdfSolarFilename,              updateFrom=startDate, updateTo=endDate,              urlTmin=NA, urlTmax=NA, urlVprp=NA, urlSolarrad=NA) #> Starting to build both netCDF files. #> ... Testing downloading of AWAP precip. grid #> ... Getting grid gemoetry from file. #> ... Deleting /home/runner/work/AWAPer/AWAPer/docs/reference/precip.20000101.grid.gz #> ... Building AWAP netcdf file. #>     NetCDF data will be updated from  2000-01-01  to  2000-01-14 #> ... Downloading non-solar data and importing to netcdf file: #> Warning: Note, the solar radiation data netCDF file will not be built or updated. #> Data construction FINISHED. #> Total run time (DD:HH:MM:SS): 00:00:00:12  # Load example catchment boundaries and remove all but the first. # Note, this is done only to speed up the example runtime. data("catchments") catchments = catchments[1,]  # Extract daily precip. data (not Tmin, Tmax, VPD, ET). # Note, the input "locations" can also be a file to a ESRI shape file. climateData = extractCatchmentData(ncdfFilename=file.names$ncdfFilename,               ncdfSolarFilename=file.names$ncdfSolarFilename,               extractFrom=startDate, extractTo=endDate,               getTmin = FALSE, getTmax = FALSE, getVprp = FALSE,               getSolarrad = FALSE, getET = FALSE,               locations=catchments,               temporal.timestep = 'daily') #> Extraction data summary: #>     NetCDF non-solar radiation climate data exists from 2000-01-01 to 2000-01-14 #>     Data will be extracted from  2000-01-01  to  2000-01-14  at  1  locations  #> Starting data extraction: #> ... Building catchment weights #> ... Starting to extract data across all locations: #> ... Calculating area weighted daily data. #> Data extraction FINISHED. #> Total run time (DD:HH:MM:SS): 00:00:00:04  # Extract the daily catchment average data. climateDataAvg = climateData$catchmentTemporal.mean  # Extract the daily catchment variance data. climateDataVar = climateData$catchmentSpatial.var  # Remove temp. files unlink(ncdfFilename) unlink(ncdfSolarFilename) # }

title: Build netCDF files of the Bureau of Meteorology (Australia) national gridded climate data. — makeNetCDF_file
text: makeNetCDF_file creates two netCDF files daily climate data. One netCDF files contains precipitation, minimum daily temperature, maximum daily temperature vappour pressure. span 1/1/1900 today requires ~20GB hard-drive space (using default compression). second netCDF file contains solar radiation started 1/1/1990 ~24GB spatial gaps infilled using 3x3 moving average repeated 3 times. minimise runtime extracting data, files stored locally network drive. Also, building files requires installation 7zip. climate data sourced  Bureau Meteorology Australian Water Availability Project (http://www.bom.gov.au/jsp/awap/.  details see Jones et al. (2009). output function required data extraction functions within package must ran prior. function can used build netCDF files stratch update existng netCDF files previously derived function. build update variable, set respective URL NA.

title: Build netCDF files of the Bureau of Meteorology (Australia) national gridded climate data. — makeNetCDF_file
code: # The example shows how to build the netCDF data cubes. # For an additional example see \url{https://github.com/peterson-tim-j/AWAPer/blob/master/README.md} #---------------------------------------  # Set dates for building netCDFs and extracting data from 15 to 5 days ago. startDate = as.Date(Sys.Date()-15,"%Y-%m-%d") endDate = as.Date(Sys.Date()-5,"%Y-%m-%d")  # Set names for netCDF files (in the system temp. directory). ncdfFilename = tempfile(fileext='.nc') ncdfSolarFilename = tempfile(fileext='.nc')  # \donttest{ # Build netCDF grids for all data but only over the defined time period. file.names = makeNetCDF_file(ncdfFilename=ncdfFilename,              ncdfSolarFilename=ncdfSolarFilename,              updateFrom=startDate, updateTo=endDate) #> Starting to build both netCDF files. #> ... Testing downloading of AWAP precip. grid #> ... Getting grid gemoetry from file. #> ... Deleting /home/runner/work/AWAPer/AWAPer/docs/reference/precip.20000101.grid.gz #> ... Testing downloading of AWAP tmin grid #> ... Testing downloading of AWAP tmax grid #> ... Testing downloading of AWAP vapour pressure grid #> ... Testing downloading of AWAP solar grid #> ... Getting grid gemoetry from file. #> ... Deleting /home/runner/work/AWAPer/AWAPer/docs/reference/solarrad.20000101.grid.gz #> ... Building AWAP netcdf file. #>     NetCDF data will be updated from  2025-11-23  to  2025-12-03 #> ... Downloading non-solar data and importing to netcdf file: #>     NetCDF Solar data will be updated from  2025-11-23  to  2025-12-03 #> ... Downloading solar data and importing to netcdf file: #> Data construction FINISHED. #> Total run time (DD:HH:MM:SS): 00:00:00:57  # Now, to demonstrate updating the netCDF grids to one day ago, rerun with # the same file names but \code{updateFrom=NA}. file.names = makeNetCDF_file(ncdfFilename=ncdfFilename,              ncdfSolarFilename=ncdfSolarFilename,              updateFrom=NA) #> Starting to update both netCDF files. #> ... Testing downloading of AWAP precip. grid #> ... Getting grid gemoetry from file. #> ... Deleting /home/runner/work/AWAPer/AWAPer/docs/reference/precip.20000101.grid.gz #> ... Testing downloading of AWAP tmin grid #> ... Testing downloading of AWAP tmax grid #> ... Testing downloading of AWAP vapour pressure grid #> ... Testing downloading of AWAP solar grid #> ... Getting grid gemoetry from file. #> ... Deleting /home/runner/work/AWAPer/AWAPer/docs/reference/solarrad.20000101.grid.gz #>     NetCDF data will be updated from  2025-12-03  to  2025-12-07 #> ... Downloading non-solar data and importing to netcdf file: #>     NetCDF Solar data will be updated from  2025-12-03  to  2025-12-07 #> ... Downloading solar data and importing to netcdf file: #> Data construction FINISHED. #> Total run time (DD:HH:MM:SS): 00:00:00:31   # Remove temp. files  unlink(ncdfFilename)  unlink(ncdfSolarFilename) # }
